# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Release & Publish

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*' # Trigger on pushes to tags that start with 'v' (e.g., v1.0.0, v1.2.3-alpha)

permissions:
  contents: write # Required to create GitHub Releases

jobs:
  release:
    name: Create GitHub Release
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0 # Required for softprops/action-gh-release to generate release notes based on commit history

      # --- Build Application (Example: Node.js) ---
      # Replace these steps with your actual build process
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 'lts/*' # Use the latest LTS version of Node.js

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci # Or 'npm install' if you don't use a lock file

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build # Replace with your actual build command (e.g., 'make build', 'python -m build')

      # --- Create GitHub Release ---
      - name: Create GitHub Release
        id: create_release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          prerelease: ${{ contains(github.ref_name, '-alpha') || contains(github.ref_name, '-beta') || contains(github.ref_name, '-rc') }}
          generate_release_notes: true # Automatically generate release notes from commit messages since the last release
          # target_commitish: ${{ github.sha }} # Optional: Use the specific commit SHA that triggered the workflow
          # body: "Detailed release notes can go here, or let generate_release_notes handle it."
          # files: | # Optional: Upload build artifacts to the release
          #   ./dist/*.zip
          #   ./build/*.tar.gz
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} # This token is automatically provided by GitHub Actions

  publish:
    name: Publish Packages
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: release # Ensure the release job completes successfully before publishing

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # --- Publish to npm (Example) ---
      # Uncomment and configure these steps if publishing to npm
      # - name: Setup Node.js for npm publish
      #   uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      #   with:
      #     node-version: 'lts/*'
      #     registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org/' # Ensure this is the correct registry URL

      # - name: Install npm dependencies
      #   run: npm ci

      # - name: Publish to npm
      #   run: npm publish
      #   env:
      #     NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }} # Create a secret named NPM_TOKEN in your repository settings with an npm publish token

      # --- Publish to PyPI (Example) ---
      # Uncomment and configure these steps if publishing to PyPI
      # - name: Setup Python for PyPI publish
      #   uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      #   with:
      #     python-version: '3.x' # Specify your Python version

      # - name: Install PyPI publish dependencies
      #   run: pip install build twine

      # - name: Build Python package
      #   run: python -m build # This will create dist/*.whl and dist/*.tar.gz

      # - name: Publish to PyPI
      #   run: twine upload --repository pypi dist/*
      #   env:
      #     TWINE_USERNAME: '__token__' # For API token authentication
      #     TWINE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.PYPI_API_TOKEN }} # Create a secret named PYPI_API_TOKEN in your repository settings with a PyPI API token

      # --- Other publishing steps (e.g., Docker, Maven, RubyGems) can be added here ---
